liquidity.label: Improving
decomp: SOFR flat at 3.63%, no funding stress

structured_conclusion.likely_rotation: Quality growth / mega-cap software over broad semis
structured_conclusion.next_catalyst: H.4.1 Fed balance sheet release — 1 day (Jul 16)
structured_conclusion.korea_gate: 1/3 — USDKRW below 1,530 PASS; EWY below SMA20 FAIL; KOSPI below 8,200 FAIL

bias: Financial conditions have eased modestly from recent tightness — bank reserves are rising and the Treasury General Account is drawing down — but the reverse repo buffer that has absorbed prior liquidity swings for three years is now nearly exhausted, and real yields remain restrictive even as nominal rates drift lower.

counterargs.thesis: Liquidity backdrop improving at the margin / early disinflationary slowdown
supporting: TGA drew down ~$145B in two weeks, injecting reserves into the system
Nominal 10Y and 2Y yields both declined this week
VIX fell to 16.17 — equity vol continues to be suppressed
against: RRP drained to $0.28B — the Fed's liquidity buffer is effectively gone
MOVE index up ~7% to 75.0 — bond vol building even as VIX falls
Oil up ~6.7%/wk to $79.56 — renewed inflation risk to the disinflation narrative
Korea gate still closed 1/3 — EWY well below its 20-day SMA and KOSPI below 8,200
Semis under pressure today (SOXX -3.86%, DRAM -8.67%) even as SK Hynix/Samsung rallied domestically; 9 enabling-layer tickers have dropped off the watchlist
counterargs.read: The plumbing keeps improving — TGA is draining, reserves are climbing, and nominal yields are drifting lower — but the RRP buffer that has cushioned liquidity swings for three years is now essentially empty, so the next TGA rebuild will show up directly in reserve balances rather than being absorbed. Combine that with oil's climb back above $79 and a semiconductor complex that sold off hard intraday even as Korean chip names rallied domestically, and the case for a clean 'liquidity expansion' regime isn't secure yet. Korea's gate stays closed on two of three conditions, reinforcing that this is a backdrop getting better at the margin, not a green light for broad risk-on rotation.

trade_table.rationale: 10Y/2Y drifting lower, breakevens flat, but RRP depletion is a lurking risk into the next TGA rebuild.
ALLY, COF rallying; HY OAS only marginally wider and still historically tight.
SOXX -3.86% and DRAM -8.67% intraday selloff; enabling-layer thesis tickers removed from watchlist this run.
Gate closed 1/3; EWY well below SMA20 despite a strong domestic KOSPI session.
Oil +6.7%/wk to $79.56 — sustained strength would threaten the disinflation thesis.

catalysts.event: H.4.1 Factors Affecting Reserve Balances release (WALCL/TGA/Reserves)
catalysts.note: Will confirm whether TGA drawdown and reserve build continued into this week

duration_score.reason: 10Y and 2Y both declined this week while breakevens held flat and HY OAS widened only marginally — consistent with a mild growth/Fed-easing read rather than the real-rate tightening pain trade seen in early July.

james_framework.what_market_IS_saying: Nominal yields drifting lower across the curve while inflation expectations hold steady — market pricing gradual disinflation, not a growth shock.
james_framework.pain_trade: The sticky-front-end pain trade flagged in prior runs has eased for now — nominal 2Y is falling, not sticky.
james_framework.watch_signal: If nominal 2Y reverses higher while breakevens fall, real-rate tightening resumes and the pain trade is back on.
